input.content:
# INDEPENDENT POSTMORTEM: Why the "cellcog" compose pipeline failed (task 72, RACE 0.2691 vs champion 0.4447)

Auditor: Fable (independent; all findings verified directly from artifacts, code, git history, and progress notes; no prior conclusion deferred to).

---

## ROOT CAUSE VERDICT (one sentence)

Cellcog failed because it replaced the act of *writing* with a closed template grammar — every attribution, transition, and "insight" sentence in the published report is stitched from roughly a dozen fixed string templates that a model never touches, and its faithfulness contract explicitly bans the causal explanation, interpretation, and contrast that the RACE judge defines as insight — so the pipeline shipped a 7,703-word machine-generated concordance of quotations that reads as broken, repetitive, and analysis-free next to the champion's ordinary flowing academic prose, and nobody discovered this because a 36-hour cascade of infrastructure failures and a self-imposed adversarial-faithfulness program meant the rebuilt pipeline's output was never once scored or even read at full size until the very end.

---

## 0. The verified score data

| | Comp | Insight | IF | Read | Overall | words |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| cellcog first publish (per brief) | 0.1998 | 0.1861 | 0.2306 | 0.2353 | 0.2083 | 1,881 |
| cellcog full fix (`flywheel/third_party/deep_research_bench/results/race/cellcog_7703/race_result.txt`, verified) | 0.3167 | 0.2336 | 0.2847 | 0.2153 | 0.2691 | 7,703 |
| champion (`outline_agent/third_party/deep_research_bench/results/race/polaris_step3_control/race_result.txt`, verified) | 0.4569 | 0.4293 | 0.4587 | 0.4310 | 0.4447 | 3,875 |
| stock claude-3-7-sonnet (same harness) | 0.4110 | 0.4051 | 0.4621 | 0.4172 | 0.4218 | — |
| namesake competitor "cellcog(-max)" (measured 07-13, FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:262-265) | 9.53/10 | 9.48/10 | 9.24/10 | 9.11/10 | **0.5603** | 13,580 |

RACE mechanics (verified in `score_prompt_en.py`): the judge reads target and reference **side by side** in one call, scores each 0–10 per weighted criterion; final = target/(target+reference). Cellcog's Readability 0.2153 implies the judge scored it ≈ 0.27× the reference — roughly **2.2/10, the judge's "poor / very poor" band** — while sitting next to a fluent reference report. The champion's 0.4310 implies ≈ 6.1/10. Note that even a *stock single-model* report scores 0.42: cellcog's 0.27 is not "below SOTA", it is far below the no-pipeline baseline.

Naming note: the pipeline is named after the leaderboard #1 system it was trying to replicate (cellcog-max, 0.5603 on task 72). The clone scored 0.2691 against its namesake's 0.5603 — and, as §2/§3 show, the reason is that the clone copied the champion's *countable surface features* and outlawed its *substance*.

---

## 1. PROSE / WRITING QUALITY — the core failure, quantified

### 1.1 What cellcog's prose actually is

Read `flywheel/outputs/release/report.md` end to end and a precise characterization emerges: **it is not prose at all; it is a database join rendered as sentences.** Every sentence is one of four machine kinds:

1. **Attribution-template sentence**: one of 8 closed templates (`report_ast.py:616-627`) prefixed to a verbatim span quote —
   > "The evidence Zivko Krstic present in the Croatian Regional Development Journal in 2024 is that skill-biased technological change…" (report.md:42)
2. **Level-disclosure transition**: one of ~8 fixed phrasings (`cohesion_pass.py:198-224`) —
   > "The evidence to this point is measured at the worker level; what follows is measured at the industry level." (report.md:25 and 17 more times)
3. **Template verdict**: one of ~9 canned "insight" strings (`argument_planner.py:1187-1249`) —
   > "The unit labels are declared metadata and are not fully stated in the quoted findings, so this is an indirect comparison of employment across different units of analysis (worker versus economy) and is not directly comparable." (report.md:98 — **seven times consecutively in one section, verbatim except the parenthetical**)
4. **LLM-written OWNED sentence**, but under a contract that bans it from carrying "a number, a name, or a new particular" or "a causal explanation" (`cellcog_composer.py` WRITE_PROMPT rule 4), so it can only produce hedged epistemic bookkeeping ("…the evidence does not settle whether…", "…cannot distinguish…", "…remains unresolved").

### 1.2 The numbers (measured on the published artifacts)

| metric | cellcog (7,703w) | champion (3,875w) |
|---|---|---|
| sentences (body) | 147 | 97 |
| **mean words/sentence** | **48.8** (max 122) | 34.1 (max 64) |
| sentences opening with an attribution formula | **67/147 = 46%** | 0 |
| meta/scaffolding sentences (level disclosure, unit-label boilerplate, design-comparison boilerplate) | **42/147 = 29%** | 0 |
| either of the above | **74% of all sentences** | 0% |
| sentences sharing a 6-word opener with another sentence | **47** | **0** |
| " while " clause-joins | 54 | 21 |
| verbatim repeats of "The unit labels are declared metadata…" | **19** | — |
| level-disclosure transitions ("measured at the X level") | 18 | — |
| pure boilerplate as share of body words | **16%** | ~0% |

Template-instance counts in the final report: "writing in {venue} in {year}" ×38, "The evidence {who} present in {venue}…is that" ×13, "{who} establish, in {venue}…" ×13, "{who} find, in their {year} {venue} paper" ×12, "A {year} {venue} study by {who} finds that" ×13. **That is ~114 sentences of a ~147-sentence report opened or spliced by one of eight fixed strings.** The brief's estimate "~1 in 5 sentences is level-disclosure meta-commentary" is *conservative*: I measure 29% of sentences as meta/boilerplate.

### 1.3 Structural and surface defects (each verified at the cited line of report.md)

- **An empty section**: `### Evidence for displacement at the occupational level` (line 84) contains **zero text** — heading directly followed by the next heading. In a section titled "Employment, Displacement, and Job Creation", the displacement evidence is literally missing.
- **Two sections that are ~100% boilerplate**: "Why firm-level and aggregate estimates diverge" (line 96-98) is seven consecutive verbatim "unit labels are declared metadata" sentences; "Where the literature genuinely disagrees" (line 150-152) is six more, and it contains **no disagreement** — the section that promises the review's adjudication delivers only comparability disclaimers.
- **Grammar produced by template slot-filling**: "The efficiency finding **in studies of improve** rests on **a observational design** in one case…" (line 170) — the `{ctx}` slot in `argument_planner.py:1190` (`f' in studies of {o2}'`) was filled with the verbatim span surface form "improve" (lifted for anchoring per `_anchor_forms`, argument_planner.py:1161-1184), and nothing checked grammar.
- **Raw PDF-extraction debris shipped verbatim** (a direct consequence of every clause being byte-bound to a span): a quote truncated mid-parenthesis — "…because they are unique **(0,** while…" (line 60); hyphenation artifacts "dramati- cally" (line 66), "self- employed" (line 72); "data Scientist" mid-sentence (line 104); a table cell truncated mid-word "BMC Medical Informatics and **Decisi**" (line 35).
- **Duplicated findings**: Acemoglu-Restrepo's 0.48%/0.47% displacement/reinstatement figures appear twice nearly verbatim (lines 44 and 48); Balloch's 100%-vs-66% clinician figures appear in the table and twice in text; Cazzaniga's 2.4%/2.2% earnings figures three times.
- **Wildly off-topic content in a labor-market review** — chronic pelvic pain prevalence, FDA sodium-reformulation QALYs, the GPTS paranoia scale (which also occupies a row of the showcase "Table 1"), COMPAS recidivism scoring, climate-policy discount rates, a loneliness meta-analysis, LitCovid curation (all in lines 32, 60, 132, 136). The paragraph at line 60 is an incoherent tour of five different disciplines glued by "while". Nothing in the composer checks topical relevance — only span fidelity — so a faithful quote about pelvic pain is publishable.
- **No narrative anywhere**: no introduction, no real conclusion; the report opens with a methods-policy paragraph pasted twice (Abstract "Methods." and "Source policy.", lines 7 and 11, near-verbatim duplicates) and ends mid-boilerplate ("…is an indirect comparison of efficiency across different units of analysis (firm versus worker) and is not directly comparable.").
- **The task's core frame is absent**: "Fourth Industrial Revolution" occurs exactly twice — in the title and one H2 — and is never discussed. That H2's only subsection body is one abstract sentence (line 19).

### 1.4 What the champion does instead

`outline_agent/docs/step3_insight_lever_ab/control_report.md` is ordinary academic prose written freely by an LLM from an evidence menu and then filtered by `strict_verify` (sentence-level NLI against spans; unverifiable sentences dropped — historically 35-41% drop rate). Concretely:

- It opens with a genuine framed introduction (Schwab, 4IR characterization, velocity/scope/systems-impact — directly answering the task prompt), then moves theory → exposure → empirics → productivity → inequality → policy → cross-study synthesis → gaps: **an argument with an arc**.
- Sentences vary in construction; attribution appears naturally and sparingly ("Acemoglu and Restrepo's task-based framework posits that…", "In a preregistered online experiment, Noy et al. (2023) assigned…"); numbers are woven into claims rather than being the claims.
- It contains real synthesis sentences of exactly the kind cellcog's contract prohibits: "These patterns imply that AI has the potential to exacerbate labor market polarization, as the benefits accrue disproportionately to workers already positioned at the upper end…" — an interpretive causal claim in the reviewer's voice.
- It is *not* flawless — some sections open abruptly mid-argument ("However, in occupations where computer use is high…" opens a section; an artifact of sentence-dropping), citations are bracket markers the RACE cleaner deletes. It still scores ~2× cellcog on every dimension. **The judge tolerates imperfect flow; it savages template monotony**, especially in a side-by-side comparative read against a fluent reference.

### 1.5 Fundamental or fixable?

The surface repetition (8 templates → 40 templates) is cosmetically fixable. The failure is not. Four load-bearing design commitments make good prose impossible *in this architecture*:

1. Every attributed clause must be span-verbatim (figures byte-checked) → the report can only be a quotation mosaic.
2. Attribution is rendered by the machine, never written by the model (`report_ast.py:609`, `render_attribution` docstring: "THE MODEL NEVER SEES THIS STRING BEFORE IT WRITES, AND NEVER TYPES IT") → uniform citational cadence, 46% of sentences.
3. The reviewer's voice may not carry particulars or mechanisms (WRITE_PROMPT rule 4: "You may NOT invent a causal explanation… you may NOT say why the world behaves that way") → insight, as RACE defines it, is contractually prohibited.
4. Transitions may only state document structure (`cohesion_pass.py:188-256`: "the transition may only STATE THE MOVE", `_frame_ok` rejects any sentence with relational vocabulary — agree/conflict/stronger/mechanism/…) → connective tissue is information-free by construction.

Fixing this is not tuning; it is inverting the architecture to the champion's shape (write freely, verify after). The style is FUNDAMENTAL to cellcog's design.

## 2. ARCHITECTURE → PROSE: the causal trace (file:line)

The pipeline: `cellcog_composer.py` (planner/writer/orchestrator) → `report_ast.py` (typed sentence-node AST + gates) → `argument_planner.py` + `synthesis_contract.py` (typed cross-source verdicts) → `cohesion_pass.py` (transitions) → `publisher.py` (0555 release boundary, publish-time re-validation).

1. **The writer emits JSON, not text.** `cellcog_composer.py` WRITE_PROMPT (line ~351-417): the model returns an array of `{voice: ATTRIBUTED, clauses:[{card_id, text}], connective}` / `{voice: OWNED, text, premise_ids}` objects. `_nodes_from` (line ~423) drops anything malformed: "Nothing is repaired, and nothing untyped ever becomes prose."
2. **Attribution is stamped on afterwards** from `_LEAD_FORMS` (5 templates) and `_TRAIL_FORMS` (3), `report_ast.py:616-627`, cycled by `form % len(forms)`. Design rationale in comments at :612-615: the RACE ArticleCleaner deletes all 281 "(YYYY)" parentheticals and `[n]` markers, so attribution was re-rendered as prose *to survive the cleaner*. The chosen fix — machine-rendered prose attribution — created 114 template sentences.
3. **Contrast is stripped.** `cellcog_composer.py:449-455`: "A contrastive connective asserts a relation no span-check proves; the writer may not. Anything not a NEUTRAL joiner collapses to 'while'." Hence 54 "while" joins, run-on sentences up to 122 words, and *no adversative argumentation anywhere in the report* — a literature review with no "however/but/in contrast" at clause level.
4. **"Insight" is a closed enum.** `argument_planner.py:_verdict_text` (1187-1239): the six verdict kinds (SAME_OUTCOME_DIFFERENT_UNIT, SAME_UNIT_OPPOSITE_DIRECTION, SAME_FINDING_DIFFERENT_METHOD, SAME_OUTCOME_DIFFERENT_HORIZON, UNCOUNTERED, + boundary text at 1242) each render ONE fixed sentence. The comment at 1156-1159 is explicit: verdicts are "Built ONLY from declared field names and surface forms lifted from the spans, so they carry no new particular BY CONSTRUCTION." Worse, the degenerate fallback (1194-1197, when unit spans aren't span-bound) emits the "unit labels are declared metadata" disclaimer — and the final report hits that fallback **19 times**, meaning most planned comparisons degenerated to the apology template.
5. **Transitions are structure-only.** `cohesion_pass.py:198-217` TEMPLATES: two phrasings per facet ("A refrain reads worse than a gap" — the author *knew*), and `_frame_ok` (:246) positively requires a document-structure subject and rejects relational vocabulary. So the 18 level-disclosure sentences are the *only* connective tissue the architecture permits.
6. **Publish-time deletion, never repair.** WRITE_PROMPT: "every [rule] is enforced mechanically, and a violating sentence is DELETED, never repaired." Combined with gates on headings (`_heading_is_proposition`, report_ast.py:1333/1941) and sidecar sentence-identity checks, whole planned units vanish (the empty H3, the all-boilerplate sections) with no smoothing pass allowed to notice.

**Contrast (champion)**: `outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py` prompts glm-5.2 to write full flowing sections (10-18+ sentence targets) with `[CITE:ev_x]` markers over a per-section evidence menu; `provenance_generator.strict_verify` (:3598) + `entailment_judge` then NLI-check each sentence against its spans and drop failures. Faithfulness is a *filter on free writing*, not a *grammar of admissible sentences*. Same faithfulness goal, ~2× the score.

**The bitterest architectural irony**: WRITE_PROMPT rule 2 cites the namesake — "The #1 system on this benchmark reports 202 quantitative findings" — while rule 4 bans exactly what the team had *already documented* (FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md 07-13 15:00, lines 234-238) as the #1 system's actual winning move: "cellcog DOES assert novel mechanisms that no source states… my prescribed fix IS STILL TOO STRICT TO REPRODUCE cellcog. It would DELETE cellcog's single highest-value section." **They wrote down that the rule would destroy the champion behavior, then built a stricter version of the rule.** The clone encoded the champion's countable features (quantitative density, in-prose journal attribution, methods narration, evidence table, H3/H4 nesting) and prohibited its uncountable one (interpretive synthesis: the judge had literally listed cellcog-max's "eight named syntheses… genuine synthesis beyond summary", scoring it 9.8/10 on insight — FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:276).

## 3. THE STRATEGIC DRIFT: from "score 0.56" to "survive 1,295 attacks"

Reconstructed timeline (git `--date=format` on `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel`, FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md):

- **07-13 07:10-16:20** — Correct diagnosis phase. Competitor forensics: cellcog-max profile measured (98 journal sources, 13,580 words, in-prose attribution surviving the cleaner, 14 epistemic tags, 8 named syntheses, judge-measured 0.5603). Levers identified: prose attribution, declared source policy, quantitative texture, labeled insight. All correct.
- **07-13 08:07** (`03fedfc`) — "SYNTHESIS CONTRACT: green. Deep thinking that cannot fabricate." The faithfulness-first frame is already installed. **07-13 14:58** (`8a1d9e1`) — `cellcog_composer.py` is born *inside a FAITHFULNESS commit*.
- **07-13 17:49-19:44** — The wheel works and is measured: turn 2 = 0.4224, turn 3 = **0.4603** (`f59bc70`). The pipeline lineage is at champion level.
- **07-13 22:35-22:51** — The pivot. Adversary: "6/6 attacks succeed while the canary is 16/16 GREEN" (`4dccd15`); Sol: "STOP RELEASE. The 0.4603 is not a real number" (`f7b7855`) — real defects: working-paper text attributed to journals (`b350715`), 43% of mechanism fields fabricated (`d6107ed`). **07-13 23:21** (`018c478`): Sol Plan 4 promises "SOTA IS REACHABLE (0.520-0.580)" *via the provable pipeline* — the drift is now sanctioned as the fast path to the score.
- **07-14 00:03 → 07-14 23:58** — Twenty-four hours, ~20 commits, **every single one adversarial-faithfulness engineering**: 0555 release boundary (`8919562`), publisher refusing its own report twice (`9c65aeb` — logged with pride), 8 "fabrication burns" (`ee156ac`), Sol overfit audits V11/V12 with a 266-vector metamorphic battery (`0418824`,`b4809b0`), culminating in "BINDING GATE: … adversary-verified (1295 attacks, 0 bypasses)" (`893c4fd`). **Zero commits in this window measure RACE. Zero read the artifact as a reader.** The last known score was the stopped 0.4603.
- **07-15 11:53** — report.md published (file mtime); **12:03** — scored: 0.2691. The first time the rebuilt pipeline's full-size output ever faced the judge — after ~36 hours of construction with no score feedback.

**Why the drift happened** (mechanism, not blame): (i) the fabrication findings were *real* (43% invented mechanisms is disqualifying), so "stop release" was correct — but the response chosen was *rebuild composition as a proof system* rather than *fix the two concrete defects and re-score*; (ii) the adversary game has crisp, immediately-checkable wins ("attacks held: 27/29") while the score is slow, noisy, and requires a publish — so every marginal hour flowed to the gate; (iii) Sol's Plan-4 promise (0.52-0.58 via the provable route) removed the felt need to keep the judge in the loop; (iv) publish-blocking gates meant no artifact existed to score even if someone had tried; (v) the champion at 0.4447 sat in a sibling worktree the whole time and was never used as a live comparison baseline for the compose work.

**What it cost**: ~36 hours; a pipeline that went 0.4603 → 0.2083 → 0.2691; and an "adversary-proof" gate whose product value is zero for the stated mission (RACE's judge "cannot SEE verifiability. It sees evidentiary TEXTURE" — the team's own note, FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:242, written *before* the drift).

**When did cellcog diverge from the champion lineage?** Architecturally at birth (07-13 08:07/14:58, the synthesis-contract + composer commits: "every sentence is either ATTRIBUTED or OWNED", `ab65cdd`); strategically at 22:51 on 07-13, when the goal function silently changed from "beat 0.5603" to "prove non-fabrication".

## 4. THE TECHNICAL FAILURE CASCADE — five failures that stacked and hid the prose problem

Each verified in code/comments (the fixes are the uncommitted 07-15 diff: +1,139/−198 across the five pipeline files):

1. **OpenRouter/glm-5.2 SSE keep-alive hangs.** `openrouter_client.py:866-881` (fix comment): OpenRouter trickles ": OPENROUTER PROCESSING" keep-alive comments that reset byte-level read timeouts, so a *dead provider stream looks alive forever*; conversely the composer's original fixed **180s wrapper timeout** "abandoned 18 whole subsection calls mid-reasoning and starved the report" (`cellcog_composer.py:89-92` fix comment — glm-5.2 is reasoning-first and needs minutes per dense section). A related mode: no reasoning cap meant "64k chars of reasoning, 0 findings written" (`cellcog_composer.py:85-88`). **Effect**: empty/gutted subsections → the 1,881-word thin first publish.
2. **The hardcoded [:12] findings cap.** `cellcog_composer.py:72-79` (fix comment): "This used to be a hard 12 — but the fact-use ledger LICENSES ~30-31 findings per subsection, so ~19 of every ~31 were silently dropped before the writer ever saw them, and the report came out 4x too thin." Now `FINDINGS_PER_SUBSECTION=40`.
3. **Sentence-splitter crashes on period-bearing tokens.** `report_ast.py:657-668` (fix comment): venue "Cureus.", author initials "Md."/"DB.", bold label "**Methods.**" plant false sentence boundaries; the publisher's sidecar identity check then aborts with `RENDERER_PRODUCED_NONCANONICAL_SENTENCE`. "There are 26 distinct period-forms in task-72 alone." Fixed by `_display_text` stripping non-terminal periods at render time.
4. **HEADING_IS_A_PROPOSITION publish block.** After removing the publish fail-open, the heading style judge (`report_ast.py:1333,1941,1365`) could block the entire publish over a heading's phrasing; `set_publish_heading_failopen` (:934) was added to scope fail-open to headings only. Commit `9c65aeb` ("the publisher REFUSED my own report, twice — and both refusals were correct") records publish-blocking being experienced as gate *success*.
5. **Empty subsections from SSE gutting** — the compound of (1)+(2): sections whose writer calls died or were starved rendered as headings with little or no body; the released report *still* contains one fully empty H3 (report.md:84) and two boilerplate-only sections.

**Why they stacked and masked the real problem**: every failure produced the same visible symptom — *a thin or unpublishable report* — and thinness is a plausible, fixable, infrastructural explanation for a bad score. So each fix ("uncap, extend timeout, sanitize periods, fail-open headings") credibly promised the next score would be fine, and the 0.2083 first score was attributed to thinness rather than to prose. Meanwhile the gate architecture ensured there was **no cheap way to look at partial output**: sentences that violated anything were deleted; publishes were refused; a full-size artifact did not exist until 07-15 morning. The one diagnostic that would have caught the real failure in five minutes — *a human (or model) reading one full section as a reader* — had no slot in the loop. When the 7,703-word artifact finally existed, the night was over: 0.2691, and Readability went **down** (0.2353 → 0.2153) as the templates were multiplied by 4× the words — direct proof the problem was never thinness.

## 5. THE SCORE, DIMENSION BY DIMENSION (grounded in `criteria_prompt_en.py`)

**Comprehensiveness 0.3167 (best-but-low).** The criteria reward breadth/depth/relevance of coverage and "breadth and quality of data sources". 820 cards did put many quantitative facts and an evidence table on the page — this is why Comp is cellcog's best dimension and the only one the "full fix" meaningfully moved (0.20→0.32). But card count ≠ field coverage: the report cites **none** of the canonical literature the task implies — Frey & Osborne 0, Autor 0, Brynjolfsson 0, Eloundou/"GPTs are GPTs" 0, Felten 0, Webb 0, Noy 0 (Acemoglu-Restrepo twice, Goos once) — while giving whole paragraphs to the Bodhi International Journal, JOURNAL GLOBAL VALUES, and the Journal of UUNT Informatics. (The champion cites Acemoglu-Restrepo, Autor, Noy, Eloundou, the OECD, ILO, IMF, St. Louis Fed.) Off-topic sources (pelvic pain, sodium/QALY, paranoia scales, COMPAS, climate discounting) *reduce* judged relevance-of-coverage. An empty displacement section and a JOURNAL_ONLY source policy that excluded the field's working-paper canon (Webb, NBER) complete the picture: enormous input breadth, weak judged breadth.

**Insight 0.2336.** The insight criteria demand depth of causal analysis, mechanisms, non-obvious/second-order effects, originality, forward outlook. Cellcog's contract prohibits items 1-2 outright (WRITE_PROMPT rule 4: no causal explanation, no new particulars; `_frame_ok` bans relational claims in transitions), and its permitted "insight" is nine canned meta-epistemic verdicts about units of analysis — 19 instances of which are the degenerate "not directly comparable" disclaimer. There is no thesis, no named synthesis (the namesake had eight, judge-listed), no adjudication (all contrast collapsed to "while"), no future outlook. The judge reads 45+ cross-source "verdicts" that all say the same thing: *we decline to compare*. Insight ~0.23 is the architecture working as designed.

**Instruction-Following 0.2847.** The task: "a literature review on the restructuring impact of AI on the labor market. Focus on how AI, as a key driver of the Fourth Industrial Revolution, is causing significant disruptions… Ensure the review only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles." (i) The 4IR frame — the explicit focus — appears twice, only in title/heading, never developed (the champion opens on it; the earlier judge run had already flagged "4IR as background rather than a guiding interpretive framework" as a triple-counted loss, FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:258). (ii) "High-quality" is *visibly* violated: the in-prose attribution design parades the venue of every claim, so the judge reads "International Journal of Apllied Mathematics" (typo in the venue name), "Cureus Journal of Business and Economics", "AGORA INTERNATIONAL JOURNAL…" over and over — the declared journal-only policy (which the methods section trumpets, correctly imitating the namesake's compliance-declaration lever) is contradicted 100+ times by the visible source list. Declared-but-dishonored compliance plausibly scores worse than silence. (iii) Off-topic material violates topical scope. (iv) Genre: a "literature review" implies narrative synthesis; a quotation concordance is not one. Note the champion scored IF 0.4587 with *all* its citations deleted by the cleaner — refuting the night's theory that cleaner-surviving attribution was the big IF lever; substance was.

**Readability 0.2153 (worst — below even the thin version's 0.2353).** The readability criteria: fluent language, clear structure, cohesion/transitions, information density without redundancy, clean data presentation. Against these: 48.8-word average sentences (122 max); 74% of sentences template-headed or boilerplate; 47 sentences sharing 6-word openers; 19 verbatim boilerplate repeats (7 consecutive); 18 structure-only transitions; an empty section; duplicated findings; truncated quote "(0,"; hyphenation debris; a truncated table cell; grammatical failures ("in studies of improve", "a observational design"); no intro/conclusion. Judged comparatively next to a fluent reference, this is the 0-2 "very poor" band (implied ~2.2/10). **Adding 4× more words made Readability worse** — the cleanest single proof that prose, not volume, was the binding constraint.

## 6. SALVAGEABILITY

**Keep:**
1. **The corpus/mine** (evidence_miner + acquisition: ~5,900 cards / 279 verified sources with spans, DOIs, facet metadata). Feeding it to the champion is the right call and is underway — with two mandatory caveats verified here: (a) it contains topically alien peer-reviewed material (pelvic pain, sodium/CVD, paranoia scales made the *final report*), so it must pass the outline_agent's two-pass semantic topic judge (Rank5 machinery, 97% cross-pass reproducible) before use; (b) its venue-quality skew (obscure/predatory-adjacent journals dominate; canonical works largely absent) means it needs *supplementing*, not just filtering — the JOURNAL_ONLY policy that built it excluded the field's core working-paper canon.
2. **Faithfulness verification as a POST-hoc report card**, not an in-line grammar: the improved entailment judge (facet-preservation rubric — the A03/A12 leniency findings are real and would improve the champion's `strict_verify`), the working-paper/journal identity resolution (alignment census; the "WP text attributed to journals" bug was a genuine P0 that the champion pipeline could also have), quantity/number span-checks, and the two-pass topic discriminator.
3. **The cross-source verdict IDEA** (typed SAME_OUTCOME_DIFFERENT_UNIT / OPPOSITE_DIRECTION / DIFFERENT_METHOD comparison *planning*) — genuinely good analytical structure **if the rendering is handed to an LLM to write naturally and then verified**, instead of rendered from closed templates.
4. Infrastructure paid for in blood: the SSE meaningful-progress watchdog, the reasoning-token cap, the period-sanitizer, the 900s writer deadline — all generally applicable to glm-5.2 pipelines.
5. The evidence-table concept (an earlier judge run twice named its absence) — with clean rendering.

**Dead ends (do not carry forward):**
1. **The compose/render/attribution architecture** — sentence-node AST as the unit of composition, machine-rendered attribution templates, template verdict rendering, structure-only cohesion pass, delete-never-repair publishing. The 0.27-vs-0.44 controlled comparison is decisive.
2. **The adversary-proof binding gate as a product goal.** "1,295 attacks / 0 bypasses" defends against a threat model (a hostile model deliberately smuggling fabrications) that is a non-goal for the mission; the champion's verify-and-drop achieves the honesty that matters at ~2× the score.
3. The "attribution must survive the RACE cleaner" lever as implemented: correct observation, wrong execution — prose attribution helps only when the venues showcased are good and the sentences remain sentences.

---

## 7. LESSONS

**(a) What the RACE judge actually rewards.** It is a comparative LLM reader, not a verifier: it rewards *texture* (visible evidentiary richness), *declared and honored* compliance, *labeled, named, original synthesis*, a guiding interpretive frame matched to the prompt, and fluent varied prose — and it punishes monotony brutally because it reads the target side-by-side with a fluent reference (and a strong report also drags the reference's score down; every improvement pays twice, FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:269). It cannot perceive provable faithfulness at all ("Being more verifiable buys nothing the judge can perceive" — the team's own pre-drift note). More words help only when they are more *analysis*: cellcog-max wins at 13.5k words of mostly-analysis; cellcog-clone lost ground going 1,881 → 7,703 words of quotation.

**(b) Faithfulness vs readability is a false dilemma at the point chosen.** The champion holds a hard faithfulness gate (strict_verify drops ~35-41% of drafted sentences) at 0.43-0.44 — faithful AND readable. The trade only becomes brutal when faithfulness is enforced *by construction* (a grammar of admissible sentences) rather than *by filtration* (free writing, then verify). Prove-while-writing cost ~0.17 overall score and bought nothing the judge can see. If a claim class can't be verified, the champion's answer (drop it, or hedge-and-label it as the namesake does) beats the clone's answer (forbid the whole class of sentence).

**(c) Process.** (1) **Never iterate 36 hours on a pipeline without scoring its output** — the score is the only ground truth, and cellcog was rebuilt end-to-end between measurements 0.4603 and 0.2083. (2) **Keep the champion in the loop as a live baseline** — it sat in a sibling worktree at 0.4447 the entire night; one side-by-side read of a single section would have exposed the prose failure instantly. (3) **Read the artifact as a reader**; every gate in the system read sentences as claims, none read the document as a text. (4) When a consultant reframes the goal (fabrication-P0 → adversary-proof gate), fix the named defects and re-anchor to the mission metric; do not let the crisper game (attacks held) displace the noisier one (score). (5) Publish-blocking style gates are score-destroying: an imperfect published report scores ~0.4; a refused perfect one scores 0. (6) The failure was foreseen in the team's own notes *twice* (07-13 15:00: the strict rule "would DELETE cellcog's single highest-value section"; cohesion_pass comment: "A refrain reads worse than a gap") — write-downs must be re-read before architecture is chosen.

**(d) The meta-lesson from this specific clone.** Cellcog-the-clone was built from a checklist of the champion's *measurable* surface features (202 quantitative findings, 9.8/1k in-prose attributions, methods narration, H4 nesting, tables) while its faithfulness contract outlawed the champion's *unmeasurable* core (interpretive, hedged, labeled synthesis in the reviewer's own voice). You cannot ablate a winner into a feature list, keep the features, ban the writing, and expect the score to survive: the writing was the product.

toolUseResult.content:
# INDEPENDENT POSTMORTEM: Why the "cellcog" compose pipeline failed (task 72, RACE 0.2691 vs champion 0.4447)

Auditor: Fable (independent; all findings verified directly from artifacts, code, git history, and progress notes; no prior conclusion deferred to).

---

## ROOT CAUSE VERDICT (one sentence)

Cellcog failed because it replaced the act of *writing* with a closed template grammar — every attribution, transition, and "insight" sentence in the published report is stitched from roughly a dozen fixed string templates that a model never touches, and its faithfulness contract explicitly bans the causal explanation, interpretation, and contrast that the RACE judge defines as insight — so the pipeline shipped a 7,703-word machine-generated concordance of quotations that reads as broken, repetitive, and analysis-free next to the champion's ordinary flowing academic prose, and nobody discovered this because a 36-hour cascade of infrastructure failures and a self-imposed adversarial-faithfulness program meant the rebuilt pipeline's output was never once scored or even read at full size until the very end.

---

## 0. The verified score data

| | Comp | Insight | IF | Read | Overall | words |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| cellcog first publish (per brief) | 0.1998 | 0.1861 | 0.2306 | 0.2353 | 0.2083 | 1,881 |
| cellcog full fix (`flywheel/third_party/deep_research_bench/results/race/cellcog_7703/race_result.txt`, verified) | 0.3167 | 0.2336 | 0.2847 | 0.2153 | 0.2691 | 7,703 |
| champion (`outline_agent/third_party/deep_research_bench/results/race/polaris_step3_control/race_result.txt`, verified) | 0.4569 | 0.4293 | 0.4587 | 0.4310 | 0.4447 | 3,875 |
| stock claude-3-7-sonnet (same harness) | 0.4110 | 0.4051 | 0.4621 | 0.4172 | 0.4218 | — |
| namesake competitor "cellcog(-max)" (measured 07-13, FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:262-265) | 9.53/10 | 9.48/10 | 9.24/10 | 9.11/10 | **0.5603** | 13,580 |

RACE mechanics (verified in `score_prompt_en.py`): the judge reads target and reference **side by side** in one call, scores each 0–10 per weighted criterion; final = target/(target+reference). Cellcog's Readability 0.2153 implies the judge scored it ≈ 0.27× the reference — roughly **2.2/10, the judge's "poor / very poor" band** — while sitting next to a fluent reference report. The champion's 0.4310 implies ≈ 6.1/10. Note that even a *stock single-model* report scores 0.42: cellcog's 0.27 is not "below SOTA", it is far below the no-pipeline baseline.

Naming note: the pipeline is named after the leaderboard #1 system it was trying to replicate (cellcog-max, 0.5603 on task 72). The clone scored 0.2691 against its namesake's 0.5603 — and, as §2/§3 show, the reason is that the clone copied the champion's *countable surface features* and outlawed its *substance*.

---

## 1. PROSE / WRITING QUALITY — the core failure, quantified

### 1.1 What cellcog's prose actually is

Read `flywheel/outputs/release/report.md` end to end and a precise characterization emerges: **it is not prose at all; it is a database join rendered as sentences.** Every sentence is one of four machine kinds:

1. **Attribution-template sentence**: one of 8 closed templates (`report_ast.py:616-627`) prefixed to a verbatim span quote —
   > "The evidence Zivko Krstic present in the Croatian Regional Development Journal in 2024 is that skill-biased technological change…" (report.md:42)
2. **Level-disclosure transition**: one of ~8 fixed phrasings (`cohesion_pass.py:198-224`) —
   > "The evidence to this point is measured at the worker level; what follows is measured at the industry level." (report.md:25 and 17 more times)
3. **Template verdict**: one of ~9 canned "insight" strings (`argument_planner.py:1187-1249`) —
   > "The unit labels are declared metadata and are not fully stated in the quoted findings, so this is an indirect comparison of employment across different units of analysis (worker versus economy) and is not directly comparable." (report.md:98 — **seven times consecutively in one section, verbatim except the parenthetical**)
4. **LLM-written OWNED sentence**, but under a contract that bans it from carrying "a number, a name, or a new particular" or "a causal explanation" (`cellcog_composer.py` WRITE_PROMPT rule 4), so it can only produce hedged epistemic bookkeeping ("…the evidence does not settle whether…", "…cannot distinguish…", "…remains unresolved").

### 1.2 The numbers (measured on the published artifacts)

| metric | cellcog (7,703w) | champion (3,875w) |
|---|---|---|
| sentences (body) | 147 | 97 |
| **mean words/sentence** | **48.8** (max 122) | 34.1 (max 64) |
| sentences opening with an attribution formula | **67/147 = 46%** | 0 |
| meta/scaffolding sentences (level disclosure, unit-label boilerplate, design-comparison boilerplate) | **42/147 = 29%** | 0 |
| either of the above | **74% of all sentences** | 0% |
| sentences sharing a 6-word opener with another sentence | **47** | **0** |
| " while " clause-joins | 54 | 21 |
| verbatim repeats of "The unit labels are declared metadata…" | **19** | — |
| level-disclosure transitions ("measured at the X level") | 18 | — |
| pure boilerplate as share of body words | **16%** | ~0% |

Template-instance counts in the final report: "writing in {venue} in {year}" ×38, "The evidence {who} present in {venue}…is that" ×13, "{who} establish, in {venue}…" ×13, "{who} find, in their {year} {venue} paper" ×12, "A {year} {venue} study by {who} finds that" ×13. **That is ~114 sentences of a ~147-sentence report opened or spliced by one of eight fixed strings.** The brief's estimate "~1 in 5 sentences is level-disclosure meta-commentary" is *conservative*: I measure 29% of sentences as meta/boilerplate.

### 1.3 Structural and surface defects (each verified at the cited line of report.md)

- **An empty section**: `### Evidence for displacement at the occupational level` (line 84) contains **zero text** — heading directly followed by the next heading. In a section titled "Employment, Displacement, and Job Creation", the displacement evidence is literally missing.
- **Two sections that are ~100% boilerplate**: "Why firm-level and aggregate estimates diverge" (line 96-98) is seven consecutive verbatim "unit labels are declared metadata" sentences; "Where the literature genuinely disagrees" (line 150-152) is six more, and it contains **no disagreement** — the section that promises the review's adjudication delivers only comparability disclaimers.
- **Grammar produced by template slot-filling**: "The efficiency finding **in studies of improve** rests on **a observational design** in one case…" (line 170) — the `{ctx}` slot in `argument_planner.py:1190` (`f' in studies of {o2}'`) was filled with the verbatim span surface form "improve" (lifted for anchoring per `_anchor_forms`, argument_planner.py:1161-1184), and nothing checked grammar.
- **Raw PDF-extraction debris shipped verbatim** (a direct consequence of every clause being byte-bound to a span): a quote truncated mid-parenthesis — "…because they are unique **(0,** while…" (line 60); hyphenation artifacts "dramati- cally" (line 66), "self- employed" (line 72); "data Scientist" mid-sentence (line 104); a table cell truncated mid-word "BMC Medical Informatics and **Decisi**" (line 35).
- **Duplicated findings**: Acemoglu-Restrepo's 0.48%/0.47% displacement/reinstatement figures appear twice nearly verbatim (lines 44 and 48); Balloch's 100%-vs-66% clinician figures appear in the table and twice in text; Cazzaniga's 2.4%/2.2% earnings figures three times.
- **Wildly off-topic content in a labor-market review** — chronic pelvic pain prevalence, FDA sodium-reformulation QALYs, the GPTS paranoia scale (which also occupies a row of the showcase "Table 1"), COMPAS recidivism scoring, climate-policy discount rates, a loneliness meta-analysis, LitCovid curation (all in lines 32, 60, 132, 136). The paragraph at line 60 is an incoherent tour of five different disciplines glued by "while". Nothing in the composer checks topical relevance — only span fidelity — so a faithful quote about pelvic pain is publishable.
- **No narrative anywhere**: no introduction, no real conclusion; the report opens with a methods-policy paragraph pasted twice (Abstract "Methods." and "Source policy.", lines 7 and 11, near-verbatim duplicates) and ends mid-boilerplate ("…is an indirect comparison of efficiency across different units of analysis (firm versus worker) and is not directly comparable.").
- **The task's core frame is absent**: "Fourth Industrial Revolution" occurs exactly twice — in the title and one H2 — and is never discussed. That H2's only subsection body is one abstract sentence (line 19).

### 1.4 What the champion does instead

`outline_agent/docs/step3_insight_lever_ab/control_report.md` is ordinary academic prose written freely by an LLM from an evidence menu and then filtered by `strict_verify` (sentence-level NLI against spans; unverifiable sentences dropped — historically 35-41% drop rate). Concretely:

- It opens with a genuine framed introduction (Schwab, 4IR characterization, velocity/scope/systems-impact — directly answering the task prompt), then moves theory → exposure → empirics → productivity → inequality → policy → cross-study synthesis → gaps: **an argument with an arc**.
- Sentences vary in construction; attribution appears naturally and sparingly ("Acemoglu and Restrepo's task-based framework posits that…", "In a preregistered online experiment, Noy et al. (2023) assigned…"); numbers are woven into claims rather than being the claims.
- It contains real synthesis sentences of exactly the kind cellcog's contract prohibits: "These patterns imply that AI has the potential to exacerbate labor market polarization, as the benefits accrue disproportionately to workers already positioned at the upper end…" — an interpretive causal claim in the reviewer's voice.
- It is *not* flawless — some sections open abruptly mid-argument ("However, in occupations where computer use is high…" opens a section; an artifact of sentence-dropping), citations are bracket markers the RACE cleaner deletes. It still scores ~2× cellcog on every dimension. **The judge tolerates imperfect flow; it savages template monotony**, especially in a side-by-side comparative read against a fluent reference.

### 1.5 Fundamental or fixable?

The surface repetition (8 templates → 40 templates) is cosmetically fixable. The failure is not. Four load-bearing design commitments make good prose impossible *in this architecture*:

1. Every attributed clause must be span-verbatim (figures byte-checked) → the report can only be a quotation mosaic.
2. Attribution is rendered by the machine, never written by the model (`report_ast.py:609`, `render_attribution` docstring: "THE MODEL NEVER SEES THIS STRING BEFORE IT WRITES, AND NEVER TYPES IT") → uniform citational cadence, 46% of sentences.
3. The reviewer's voice may not carry particulars or mechanisms (WRITE_PROMPT rule 4: "You may NOT invent a causal explanation… you may NOT say why the world behaves that way") → insight, as RACE defines it, is contractually prohibited.
4. Transitions may only state document structure (`cohesion_pass.py:188-256`: "the transition may only STATE THE MOVE", `_frame_ok` rejects any sentence with relational vocabulary — agree/conflict/stronger/mechanism/…) → connective tissue is information-free by construction.

Fixing this is not tuning; it is inverting the architecture to the champion's shape (write freely, verify after). The style is FUNDAMENTAL to cellcog's design.

---

## 2. ARCHITECTURE → PROSE: the causal trace (file:line)

The pipeline: `cellcog_composer.py` (planner/writer/orchestrator) → `report_ast.py` (typed sentence-node AST + gates) → `argument_planner.py` + `synthesis_contract.py` (typed cross-source verdicts) → `cohesion_pass.py` (transitions) → `publisher.py` (0555 release boundary, publish-time re-validation).

1. **The writer emits JSON, not text.** `cellcog_composer.py` WRITE_PROMPT (line ~351-417): the model returns an array of `{voice: ATTRIBUTED, clauses:[{card_id, text}], connective}` / `{voice: OWNED, text, premise_ids}` objects. `_nodes_from` (line ~423) drops anything malformed: "Nothing is repaired, and nothing untyped ever becomes prose."
2. **Attribution is stamped on afterwards** from `_LEAD_FORMS` (5 templates) and `_TRAIL_FORMS` (3), `report_ast.py:616-627`, cycled by `form % len(forms)`. Design rationale in comments at :612-615: the RACE ArticleCleaner deletes all 281 "(YYYY)" parentheticals and `[n]` markers, so attribution was re-rendered as prose *to survive the cleaner*. The chosen fix — machine-rendered prose attribution — created 114 template sentences.
3. **Contrast is stripped.** `cellcog_composer.py:449-455`: "A contrastive connective asserts a relation no span-check proves; the writer may not. Anything not a NEUTRAL joiner collapses to 'while'." Hence 54 "while" joins, run-on sentences up to 122 words, and *no adversative argumentation anywhere in the report* — a literature review with no "however/but/in contrast" at clause level.
4. **"Insight" is a closed enum.** `argument_planner.py:_verdict_text` (1187-1239): the six verdict kinds (SAME_OUTCOME_DIFFERENT_UNIT, SAME_UNIT_OPPOSITE_DIRECTION, SAME_FINDING_DIFFERENT_METHOD, SAME_OUTCOME_DIFFERENT_HORIZON, UNCOUNTERED, + boundary text at 1242) each render ONE fixed sentence. The comment at 1156-1159 is explicit: verdicts are "Built ONLY from declared field names and surface forms lifted from the spans, so they carry no new particular BY CONSTRUCTION." Worse, the degenerate fallback (1194-1197, when unit spans aren't span-bound) emits the "unit labels are declared metadata" disclaimer — and the final report hits that fallback **19 times**, meaning most planned comparisons degenerated to the apology template.
5. **Transitions are structure-only.** `cohesion_pass.py:198-217` TEMPLATES: two phrasings per facet ("A refrain reads worse than a gap" — the author *knew*), and `_frame_ok` (:246) positively requires a document-structure subject and rejects relational vocabulary. So the 18 level-disclosure sentences are the *only* connective tissue the architecture permits.
6. **Publish-time deletion, never repair.** WRITE_PROMPT: "every [rule] is enforced mechanically, and a violating sentence is DELETED, never repaired." Combined with gates on headings (`_heading_is_proposition`, report_ast.py:1333/1941) and sidecar sentence-identity checks, whole planned units vanish (the empty H3, the all-boilerplate sections) with no smoothing pass allowed to notice.

**Contrast (champion)**: `outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py` prompts glm-5.2 to write full flowing sections (10-18+ sentence targets) with `[CITE:ev_x]` markers over a per-section evidence menu; `provenance_generator.strict_verify` (:3598) + `entailment_judge` then NLI-check each sentence against its spans and drop failures. Faithfulness is a *filter on free writing*, not a *grammar of admissible sentences*. Same faithfulness goal, ~2× the score.

**The bitterest architectural irony**: WRITE_PROMPT rule 2 cites the namesake — "The #1 system on this benchmark reports 202 quantitative findings" — while rule 4 bans exactly what the team had *already documented* (FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md 07-13 15:00, lines 234-238) as the #1 system's actual winning move: "cellcog DOES assert novel mechanisms that no source states… my prescribed fix IS STILL TOO STRICT TO REPRODUCE cellcog. It would DELETE cellcog's single highest-value section." **They wrote down that the rule would destroy the champion behavior, then built a stricter version of the rule.** The clone encoded the champion's countable features (quantitative density, in-prose journal attribution, methods narration, evidence table, H3/H4 nesting) and prohibited its uncountable one (interpretive synthesis: the judge had literally listed cellcog-max's "eight named syntheses… genuine synthesis beyond summary", scoring it 9.8/10 on insight — FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:276).

---

## 3. THE STRATEGIC DRIFT: from "score 0.56" to "survive 1,295 attacks"

Reconstructed timeline (git `--date=format` on `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel`, FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md):

- **07-13 07:10-16:20** — Correct diagnosis phase. Competitor forensics: cellcog-max profile measured (98 journal sources, 13,580 words, in-prose attribution surviving the cleaner, 14 epistemic tags, 8 named syntheses, judge-measured 0.5603). Levers identified: prose attribution, declared source policy, quantitative texture, labeled insight. All correct.
- **07-13 08:07** (`03fedfc`) — "SYNTHESIS CONTRACT: green. Deep thinking that cannot fabricate." The faithfulness-first frame is already installed. **07-13 14:58** (`8a1d9e1`) — `cellcog_composer.py` is born *inside a FAITHFULNESS commit*.
- **07-13 17:49-19:44** — The wheel works and is measured: turn 2 = 0.4224, turn 3 = **0.4603** (`f59bc70`). The pipeline lineage is at champion level.
- **07-13 22:35-22:51** — The pivot. Adversary: "6/6 attacks succeed while the canary is 16/16 GREEN" (`4dccd15`); Sol: "STOP RELEASE. The 0.4603 is not a real number" (`f7b7855`) — real defects: working-paper text attributed to journals (`b350715`), 43% of mechanism fields fabricated (`d6107ed`). **07-13 23:21** (`018c478`): Sol Plan 4 promises "SOTA IS REACHABLE (0.520-0.580)" *via the provable pipeline* — the drift is now sanctioned as the fast path to the score.
- **07-14 00:03 → 07-14 23:58** — Twenty-four hours, ~20 commits, **every single one adversarial-faithfulness engineering**: 0555 release boundary (`8919562`), publisher refusing its own report twice (`9c65aeb` — logged with pride), 8 "fabrication burns" (`ee156ac`), Sol overfit audits V11/V12 with a 266-vector metamorphic battery (`0418824`,`b4809b0`), culminating in "BINDING GATE: … adversary-verified (1295 attacks, 0 bypasses)" (`893c4fd`). **Zero commits in this window measure RACE. Zero read the artifact as a reader.** The last known score was the stopped 0.4603.
- **07-15 11:53** — report.md published (file mtime); **12:03** — scored: 0.2691. The first time the rebuilt pipeline's full-size output ever faced the judge — after ~36 hours of construction with no score feedback.

**Why the drift happened** (mechanism, not blame): (i) the fabrication findings were *real* (43% invented mechanisms is disqualifying), so "stop release" was correct — but the response chosen was *rebuild composition as a proof system* rather than *fix the two concrete defects and re-score*; (ii) the adversary game has crisp, immediately-checkable wins ("attacks held: 27/29") while the score is slow, noisy, and requires a publish — so every marginal hour flowed to the gate; (iii) Sol's Plan-4 promise (0.52-0.58 via the provable route) removed the felt need to keep the judge in the loop; (iv) publish-blocking gates meant no artifact existed to score even if someone had tried; (v) the champion at 0.4447 sat in a sibling worktree the whole time and was never used as a live comparison baseline for the compose work.

**What it cost**: ~36 hours; a pipeline that went 0.4603 → 0.2083 → 0.2691; and an "adversary-proof" gate whose product value is zero for the stated mission (RACE's judge "cannot SEE verifiability. It sees evidentiary TEXTURE" — the team's own note, FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:242, written *before* the drift).

**When did cellcog diverge from the champion lineage?** Architecturally at birth (07-13 08:07/14:58, the synthesis-contract + composer commits: "every sentence is either ATTRIBUTED or OWNED", `ab65cdd`); strategically at 22:51 on 07-13, when the goal function silently changed from "beat 0.5603" to "prove non-fabrication".

---

## 4. THE TECHNICAL FAILURE CASCADE — five failures that stacked and hid the prose problem

Each verified in code/comments (the fixes are the uncommitted 07-15 diff: +1,139/−198 across the five pipeline files):

1. **OpenRouter/glm-5.2 SSE keep-alive hangs.** `openrouter_client.py:866-881` (fix comment): OpenRouter trickles ": OPENROUTER PROCESSING" keep-alive comments that reset byte-level read timeouts, so a *dead provider stream looks alive forever*; conversely the composer's original fixed **180s wrapper timeout** "abandoned 18 whole subsection calls mid-reasoning and starved the report" (`cellcog_composer.py:89-92` fix comment — glm-5.2 is reasoning-first and needs minutes per dense section). A related mode: no reasoning cap meant "64k chars of reasoning, 0 findings written" (`cellcog_composer.py:85-88`). **Effect**: empty/gutted subsections → the 1,881-word thin first publish.
2. **The hardcoded [:12] findings cap.** `cellcog_composer.py:72-79` (fix comment): "This used to be a hard 12 — but the fact-use ledger LICENSES ~30-31 findings per subsection, so ~19 of every ~31 were silently dropped before the writer ever saw them, and the report came out 4x too thin." Now `FINDINGS_PER_SUBSECTION=40`.
3. **Sentence-splitter crashes on period-bearing tokens.** `report_ast.py:657-668` (fix comment): venue "Cureus.", author initials "Md."/"DB.", bold label "**Methods.**" plant false sentence boundaries; the publisher's sidecar identity check then aborts with `RENDERER_PRODUCED_NONCANONICAL_SENTENCE`. "There are 26 distinct period-forms in task-72 alone." Fixed by `_display_text` stripping non-terminal periods at render time.
4. **HEADING_IS_A_PROPOSITION publish block.** After removing the publish fail-open, the heading style judge (`report_ast.py:1333,1941,1365`) could block the entire publish over a heading's phrasing; `set_publish_heading_failopen` (:934) was added to scope fail-open to headings only. Commit `9c65aeb` ("the publisher REFUSED my own report, twice — and both refusals were correct") records publish-blocking being experienced as gate *success*.
5. **Empty subsections from SSE gutting** — the compound of (1)+(2): sections whose writer calls died or were starved rendered as headings with little or no body; the released report *still* contains one fully empty H3 (report.md:84) and two boilerplate-only sections.

**Why they stacked and masked the real problem**: every failure produced the same visible symptom — *a thin or unpublishable report* — and thinness is a plausible, fixable, infrastructural explanation for a bad score. So each fix ("uncap, extend timeout, sanitize periods, fail-open headings") credibly promised the next score would be fine, and the 0.2083 first score was attributed to thinness rather than to prose. Meanwhile the gate architecture ensured there was **no cheap way to look at partial output**: sentences that violated anything were deleted; publishes were refused; a full-size artifact did not exist until 07-15 morning. The one diagnostic that would have caught the real failure in five minutes — *a human (or model) reading one full section as a reader* — had no slot in the loop. When the 7,703-word artifact finally existed, the night was over: 0.2691, and Readability went **down** (0.2353 → 0.2153) as the templates were multiplied by 4× the words — direct proof the problem was never thinness.

---

## 5. THE SCORE, DIMENSION BY DIMENSION (grounded in `criteria_prompt_en.py`)

**Comprehensiveness 0.3167 (best-but-low).** The criteria reward breadth/depth/relevance of coverage and "breadth and quality of data sources". 820 cards did put many quantitative facts and an evidence table on the page — this is why Comp is cellcog's best dimension and the only one the "full fix" meaningfully moved (0.20→0.32). But card count ≠ field coverage: the report cites **none** of the canonical literature the task implies — Frey & Osborne 0, Autor 0, Brynjolfsson 0, Eloundou/"GPTs are GPTs" 0, Felten 0, Webb 0, Noy 0 (Acemoglu-Restrepo twice, Goos once) — while giving whole paragraphs to the Bodhi International Journal, JOURNAL GLOBAL VALUES, and the Journal of UUNT Informatics. (The champion cites Acemoglu-Restrepo, Autor, Noy, Eloundou, the OECD, ILO, IMF, St. Louis Fed.) Off-topic sources (pelvic pain, sodium/QALY, paranoia scales, COMPAS, climate discounting) *reduce* judged relevance-of-coverage. An empty displacement section and a JOURNAL_ONLY source policy that excluded the field's working-paper canon (Webb, NBER) complete the picture: enormous input breadth, weak judged breadth.

**Insight 0.2336.** The insight criteria demand depth of causal analysis, mechanisms, non-obvious/second-order effects, originality, forward outlook. Cellcog's contract prohibits items 1-2 outright (WRITE_PROMPT rule 4: no causal explanation, no new particulars; `_frame_ok` bans relational claims in transitions), and its permitted "insight" is nine canned meta-epistemic verdicts about units of analysis — 19 instances of which are the degenerate "not directly comparable" disclaimer. There is no thesis, no named synthesis (the namesake had eight, judge-listed), no adjudication (all contrast collapsed to "while"), no future outlook. The judge reads 45+ cross-source "verdicts" that all say the same thing: *we decline to compare*. Insight ~0.23 is the architecture working as designed.

**Instruction-Following 0.2847.** The task: "a literature review on the restructuring impact of AI on the labor market. Focus on how AI, as a key driver of the Fourth Industrial Revolution, is causing significant disruptions… Ensure the review only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles." (i) The 4IR frame — the explicit focus — appears twice, only in title/heading, never developed (the champion opens on it; the earlier judge run had already flagged "4IR as background rather than a guiding interpretive framework" as a triple-counted loss, FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:258). (ii) "High-quality" is *visibly* violated: the in-prose attribution design parades the venue of every claim, so the judge reads "International Journal of Apllied Mathematics" (typo in the venue name), "Cureus Journal of Business and Economics", "AGORA INTERNATIONAL JOURNAL…" over and over — the declared journal-only policy (which the methods section trumpets, correctly imitating the namesake's compliance-declaration lever) is contradicted 100+ times by the visible source list. Declared-but-dishonored compliance plausibly scores worse than silence. (iii) Off-topic material violates topical scope. (iv) Genre: a "literature review" implies narrative synthesis; a quotation concordance is not one. Note the champion scored IF 0.4587 with *all* its citations deleted by the cleaner — refuting the night's theory that cleaner-surviving attribution was the big IF lever; substance was.

**Readability 0.2153 (worst — below even the thin version's 0.2353).** The readability criteria: fluent language, clear structure, cohesion/transitions, information density without redundancy, clean data presentation. Against these: 48.8-word average sentences (122 max); 74% of sentences template-headed or boilerplate; 47 sentences sharing 6-word openers; 19 verbatim boilerplate repeats (7 consecutive); 18 structure-only transitions; an empty section; duplicated findings; truncated quote "(0,"; hyphenation debris; a truncated table cell; grammatical failures ("in studies of improve", "a observational design"); no intro/conclusion. Judged comparatively next to a fluent reference, this is the 0-2 "very poor" band (implied ~2.2/10). **Adding 4× more words made Readability worse** — the cleanest single proof that prose, not volume, was the binding constraint.

---

## 6. SALVAGEABILITY

**Keep:**
1. **The corpus/mine** (evidence_miner + acquisition: ~5,900 cards / 279 verified sources with spans, DOIs, facet metadata). Feeding it to the champion is the right call and is underway — with two mandatory caveats verified here: (a) it contains topically alien peer-reviewed material (pelvic pain, sodium/CVD, paranoia scales made the *final report*), so it must pass the outline_agent's two-pass semantic topic judge (Rank5 machinery, 97% cross-pass reproducible) before use; (b) its venue-quality skew (obscure/predatory-adjacent journals dominate; canonical works largely absent) means it needs *supplementing*, not just filtering — the JOURNAL_ONLY policy that built it excluded the field's core working-paper canon.
2. **Faithfulness verification as a POST-hoc report card**, not an in-line grammar: the improved entailment judge (facet-preservation rubric — the A03/A12 leniency findings are real and would improve the champion's `strict_verify`), the working-paper/journal identity resolution (alignment census; the "WP text attributed to journals" bug was a genuine P0 that the champion pipeline could also have), quantity/number span-checks, and the two-pass topic discriminator.
3. **The cross-source verdict IDEA** (typed SAME_OUTCOME_DIFFERENT_UNIT / OPPOSITE_DIRECTION / DIFFERENT_METHOD comparison *planning*) — genuinely good analytical structure **if the rendering is handed to an LLM to write naturally and then verified**, instead of rendered from closed templates.
4. Infrastructure paid for in blood: the SSE meaningful-progress watchdog, the reasoning-token cap, the period-sanitizer, the 900s writer deadline — all generally applicable to glm-5.2 pipelines.
5. The evidence-table concept (an earlier judge run twice named its absence) — with clean rendering.

**Dead ends (do not carry forward):**
1. **The compose/render/attribution architecture** — sentence-node AST as the unit of composition, machine-rendered attribution templates, template verdict rendering, structure-only cohesion pass, delete-never-repair publishing. The 0.27-vs-0.44 controlled comparison is decisive.
2. **The adversary-proof binding gate as a product goal.** "1,295 attacks / 0 bypasses" defends against a threat model (a hostile model deliberately smuggling fabrications) that is a non-goal for the mission; the champion's verify-and-drop achieves the honesty that matters at ~2× the score.
3. The "attribution must survive the RACE cleaner" lever as implemented: correct observation, wrong execution — prose attribution helps only when the venues showcased are good and the sentences remain sentences.

---

## 7. LESSONS

**(a) What the RACE judge actually rewards.** It is a comparative LLM reader, not a verifier: it rewards *texture* (visible evidentiary richness), *declared and honored* compliance, *labeled, named, original synthesis*, a guiding interpretive frame matched to the prompt, and fluent varied prose — and it punishes monotony brutally because it reads the target side-by-side with a fluent reference (and a strong report also drags the reference's score down; every improvement pays twice, FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:269). It cannot perceive provable faithfulness at all ("Being more verifiable buys nothing the judge can perceive" — the team's own pre-drift note). More words help only when they are more *analysis*: cellcog-max wins at 13.5k words of mostly-analysis; cellcog-clone lost ground going 1,881 → 7,703 words of quotation.

**(b) Faithfulness vs readability is a false dilemma at the point chosen.** The champion holds a hard faithfulness gate (strict_verify drops ~35-41% of drafted sentences) at 0.43-0.44 — faithful AND readable. The trade only becomes brutal when faithfulness is enforced *by construction* (a grammar of admissible sentences) rather than *by filtration* (free writing, then verify). Prove-while-writing cost ~0.17 overall score and bought nothing the judge can see. If a claim class can't be verified, the champion's answer (drop it, or hedge-and-label it as the namesake does) beats the clone's answer (forbid the whole class of sentence).

**(c) Process.** (1) **Never iterate 36 hours on a pipeline without scoring its output** — the score is the only ground truth, and cellcog was rebuilt end-to-end between measurements 0.4603 and 0.2083. (2) **Keep the champion in the loop as a live baseline** — it sat in a sibling worktree at 0.4447 the entire night; one side-by-side read of a single section would have exposed the prose failure instantly. (3) **Read the artifact as a reader**; every gate in the system read sentences as claims, none read the document as a text. (4) When a consultant reframes the goal (fabrication-P0 → adversary-proof gate), fix the named defects and re-anchor to the mission metric; do not let the crisper game (attacks held) displace the noisier one (score). (5) Publish-blocking style gates are score-destroying: an imperfect published report scores ~0.4; a refused perfect one scores 0. (6) The failure was foreseen in the team's own notes *twice* (07-13 15:00: the strict rule "would DELETE cellcog's single highest-value section"; cohesion_pass comment: "A refrain reads worse than a gap") — write-downs must be re-read before architecture is chosen.

**(d) The meta-lesson from this specific clone.** Cellcog-the-clone was built from a checklist of the champion's *measurable* surface features (202 quantitative findings, 9.8/1k in-prose attributions, methods narration, H4 nesting, tables) while its faithfulness contract outlawed the champion's *unmeasurable* core (interpretive, hedged, labeled synthesis in the reviewer's own voice). You cannot ablate a winner into a feature list, keep the features, ban the writing, and expect the score to survive: the writing was the product.

content.text:
Audit complete and written to `/tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/fable_postmortem_ANSWER.md`.

**ROOT CAUSE VERDICT:** Cellcog failed because it replaced *writing* with a closed template grammar — every attribution, transition, and "insight" sentence is stitched from ~a dozen fixed strings a model never touches, and its faithfulness contract explicitly bans the causal explanation and contrast the RACE judge defines as insight — so it shipped a 7,703-word quotation concordance that no one scored or even read at full size until the 36-hour night was already spent.

**Most important findings (all verified from artifacts/code/git):**

1. **The prose is machine-assembled, quantified:** 74% of the report's 147 sentences are template-headed or boilerplate (46% from the 8 closed attribution templates at `report_ast.py:616-627`, 29% meta-scaffolding); mean sentence 48.8 words (max 122); 19 verbatim repeats of "The unit labels are declared metadata…" (7 consecutive in one section); one fully **empty section** (report.md:84); champion comparison: 0 template sentences, 0 shared openers, mean 34 words.

2. **The style is fundamental, not fixable:** attribution is "RENDERED FROM THE GRAPH — NEVER WRITTEN BY A MODEL"; contrastive connectives are forcibly collapsed to "while" (`cellcog_composer.py:449`); transitions may only state document structure (`cohesion_pass.py:_frame_ok`); and WRITE_PROMPT rule 4 bans causal explanation — the exact behavior the RACE Insight criteria reward and that the team had *already documented* (07-13 15:00 note) as the real cellcog-max's winning move ("my prescribed fix… would DELETE cellcog's single highest-value section").

3. **The drift has a timestamp:** the pipeline was scoring 0.4603 at 07-13 19:44; Sol's "STOP RELEASE" at 22:51 (real fabrication defects) pivoted all ~20 commits of the next 24 hours into adversarial faithfulness gates ("1295 attacks, 0 bypasses"); **zero RACE measurements occurred between 0.4603 and the 0.2083/0.2691 finale** on 07-15 morning.

4. **The cascade masked the prose failure:** SSE keep-alive hangs + a 180s wrapper that "abandoned 18 whole subsection calls" + the hard-12 findings cap ("~19 of every ~31 silently dropped… 4x too thin") + splitter crashes ("Cureus.", "Md.", "DB.") + heading publish-blocks all produced the same symptom — a thin/unpublishable report — so every fix credibly blamed thinness; when 4× the words finally shipped, **Readability went DOWN** (0.2353→0.2153), proving prose was the binding constraint all along.

5. **Comprehensiveness/IF failed on substance, not surface:** 820 cards but zero citations of Frey-Osborne, Autor, Brynjolfsson, Eloundou, Felten, Webb; off-topic peer-reviewed junk (pelvic pain, sodium QALYs, paranoia scales) shipped in the report and its showcase table; the in-prose attribution design paraded low-quality venues ("International Journal of Apllied Mathematics") 100+ times against a declared "high-quality journals only" policy; the 4IR frame appears twice, only in headings. Salvage the corpus (topic-screened), the post-hoc verification machinery, and the verdict *idea* (LLM-rendered); the compose/render architecture is a dead end.
